evals.prompt: agent audit — I have an agent built on Claude API that helps users write code, can read files, run terminal commands, and edit files. It's still a prototype for a small team.
evals.expected_output: Should start Phase 1 by asking for a more detailed description or proceed to Phase 2 follow-ups since enough info was given. Should calibrate for solo developer / hobbyist persona.

evals.prompt: I want to evaluate our agent infrastructure. We have a multi-agent system running in production serving ~500 enterprise users. Agents handle document processing, data extraction, and workflow automation. We use LangGraph for orchestration, have Redis-based session persistence, and a custom tool registry. Permission system is role-based via our existing IAM.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an engineering team / production system. Should skip basic Day One questions and probe for gaps in workflow state, idempotency, token budgeting, verification harness, and Week One primitives.

evals.prompt: agent audit — I haven't built anything yet but I'm planning to create an AI assistant for a SaaS product that will answer user questions based on documentation and perform simple actions like creating tickets or updating account settings
evals.expected_output: Should switch to design mode as instructed in guardrails. Should recommend which primitives to implement first, in what order, calibrated for a solo/small-team SaaS developer.
